data.text: I'll start by reading the current lefthook.yml and checking the skill directory structure.

data.output:
1	# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
2	# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
3	# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook
4	
5	# Configuration
6	colors: true
7	no_tty: false
8	assertion: false
9	
10	# Reusable command groups
11	command_groups:
12	  go_checks: &go_checks
13	    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
14	    tags: [go, backend]
15	
16	  security_checks: &security_checks
17	    tags: [security]
18	
19	  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
20	    tags: [format, style]
21	
22	# ====================
23	# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
24	# ====================
25	pre-commit:
26	  parallel: true
27	
28	  commands:
29	    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
30	    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
31	    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
32	    conflicts:
33	      name: "Check merge conflicts"
34	      run: |
35	        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
36	        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
37	        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
38	          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
39	          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
40	        done | head -1)
41	        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
42	          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
43	          exit 1
44	        fi
45	        exit 0
46	      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."
47	
48	    git-config-health:
49	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
50	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."
51	
52	    beads-tracker-health:
53	      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
54	      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."
55	
56	    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
57	    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
58	    # debug-go:
59	    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
60	    #   glob: "*.go"
61	    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
62	    #   run: |
63	    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
64	    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
65	    #     else
66	    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
67	    #     fi
68	    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"
69	
70	    debug-python:
71	      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
72	      glob: "*.py"
73	      run: |
74	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
75	          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
76	        else
77	          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
78	        fi
79	      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"
80	
81	    # Go formatting - native gofmt
82	    go-fmt:
83	      <<: *go_checks
84	      name: "Go Format"
85	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
86	      run: |
87	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
88	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
89	          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
90	          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
91	          exit 1
92	        fi
93	      skip:
94	        - merge
95	        - rebase
96	
97	    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
98	    go-lint:
99	      <<: *go_checks
100	      name: "Go Lint"
101	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
102	      run: |
103	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
104	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
105	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
106	          exit 1
107	        fi
108	        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
109	        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
110	        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
111	        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
112	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
113	        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
114	        for pkg in $packages; do
115	          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
116	        done
117	      skip:
118	        - merge
119	        - rebase
120	
121	    # Go build - only for changed packages
122	    go-build:
123	      <<: *go_checks
124	      name: "Go Build"
125	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
126	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
127	      skip:
128	        - merge
129	        - rebase
130	
131	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
132	    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
133	    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
134	    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
135	    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
136	    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
137	    evidence-lint:
138	      <<: *go_checks
139	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
140	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
141	      run: |
142	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
143	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
144	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
145	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
146	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
147	        violations=""
148	        for f in $staged; do
149	          bare="${f#cli/}"
150	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
151	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
152	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
153	          fi
154	        done
155	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
156	          printf '%s' "$violations"
157	          exit 1
158	        fi
159	        exit 0
160	      skip:
161	        - merge
162	        - rebase
163	      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."
164	
165	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
166	    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
167	    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
168	    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
169	    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
170	    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
171	    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
172	    runtime-lint:
173	      <<: *go_checks
174	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
175	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
176	      run: |
177	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
178	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
179	        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
180	        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
181	        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
182	        violations=""
183	        for f in $staged; do
184	          bare="${f#cli/}"
185	          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
186	          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
187	            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
188	          fi
189	        done
190	        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
191	          printf '%s' "$violations"
192	          exit 1
193	        fi
194	        exit 0
195	      skip:
196	        - merge
197	        - rebase
198	      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."
199	
200	    # Go tests - targeted testing
201	    go-test:
202	      <<: *go_checks
203	      name: "Go Tests"
204	      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
205	      run: |
206	        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
207	        for pkg in $packages; do
208	          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
209	            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
210	          fi
211	        done
212	      skip:
213	        - merge
214	        - rebase
215	      env:
216	        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
217	        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"
218	
219	    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
220	    secrets:
221	      <<: *security_checks
222	      name: "Scan for secrets"
223	      run: |
224	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
225	          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
226	        else
227	          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
228	          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
229	          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')
230	
231	          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
232	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
233	            echo "Potential secrets detected"
234	            exit 1
235	          fi
236	
237	          # AWS keys
238	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
239	            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
240	            exit 1
241	          fi
242	
243	          # GitHub tokens
244	          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
245	            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
246	            exit 1
247	          fi
248	        fi
249	      exclude:
250	        - "*.md"
251	        - "go.sum"
252	        - "package-lock.json"
253	        - "*_test.go"
254	        - "**/dist/**"
255	        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
256	      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"
257	
258	    # Binary and large file check
259	    large-files:
260	      name: "Check file sizes"
261	      run: |
262	        for file in {staged_files}; do
263	          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
264	          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
265	            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
266	            exit 1
267	          fi
268	        done
269	      exclude:
270	        - "*.png"
271	        - "*.jpg"
272	        - "*.jpeg"
273	        - "*.gif"
274	        - "*.svg"
275	        - "*.ico"
276	        - "*.pdf"
277	        - "*.woff"
278	        - "*.woff2"
279	        - "*.ttf"
280	        - "*.eot"
281	        # bead store grows monotonically with queue activity; expected to exceed 5MB
282	        - ".ddx/beads.jsonl"
283	        # archive partner accumulates closed beads forever; trends past 5MB
284	        - ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"
285	      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"
286	
287	    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
288	    ddx-validate:
289	      name: "Validate DDx"
290	      run: |
291	        # Only run if DDx files are staged
292	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
293	          exit 0
294	        fi
295	
296	        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
297	        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
298	        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
299	        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
300	        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.
301	        # ddx-64ac553a Phase 0 (defensive triage; real fix is the central
302	        # git.Command wrapper in Phase 2a).
303	        unset $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars)
304	
305	        # Check for DDx command availability
306	        if command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
307	          ddx doctor >/dev/null 2>&1
308	        elif [ -x "./cli/ddx" ]; then
309	          ./cli/ddx doctor >/dev/null 2>&1
310	        fi
311	      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"
312	
313	    skill-schema:
314	      <<: *go_checks
315	      name: "Validate skill metadata"
316	      run: |
317	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
318	          exit 0
319	        fi
320	        make skill-schema
321	      skip:
322	        - merge
323	        - rebase
324	
325	    sync-embedded-skills:
326	      name: "Sync cli/internal/skills/ddx from top-level skills/ddx"
327	      root: "cli/"
328	      run: |
329	        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^skills/ddx/'; then
330	          exit 0
331	        fi
332	        make copy-skills
333	        cd .. && git add cli/internal/skills/ddx/
334	      stage_fixed: true
335	      skip:
336	        - merge
337	        - rebase
338	      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/skills/ddx from skills/ddx. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."
339	
340	    design-md-lint:
341	      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
342	      glob: ".stitch/DESIGN.md"
343	      run: |
344	        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
345	          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"
346	          exit 0
347	        fi
348	        bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || bun install
349	        bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
350	      fail_text: "DESIGN.md lint failed. Run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md"
351	
352	    test-engineer-persona-drift:
353	      name: "Guard test-engineer persona (ddx-482fad2f)"
354	      glob: "library/personas/test-engineer.md"
355	      run: |
356	        if grep -q "Mock all external dependencies" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
357	          echo "ERROR: 'Mock all external dependencies' is forbidden in the test-engineer persona (see ddx-482fad2f)"
358	          exit 1
359	        fi
360	        if grep -q "test doubles (mocks" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
361	          echo "ERROR: 'test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)' lists mocks and stubs as equivalent — forbidden (see ddx-482fad2f)"
362	          exit 1
363	        fi
364	      fail_text: "test-engineer persona contains forbidden mock-promotion phrases"
365	
366	    no-tmp-in-test-factory:
367	      name: "Guard NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") in tests (ddx-9063101c)"
368	      glob: "cli/cmd/*_test.go"
369	      run: |
370	        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/cmd/[^/]+_test\.go$' || true)
371	        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
372	        bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs grep -lF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")' 2>/dev/null || true)
373	        if [ -n "$bad" ]; then
374	          printf '%s\n' "$bad" | xargs grep -nF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")'
375	          exit 1
376	        fi
377	        exit 0
378	      fail_text: "NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") found in cli/cmd/*_test.go — use t.TempDir() instead"
379	
380	# ====================
381	# CI/CD - Full validation (all files)
382	# ====================
383	ci:
384	  parallel: true
385	
386	  commands:
387	    # Full conflict check
388	    conflicts-all:
389	      name: "Check all merge conflicts"
390	      run: |
391	        # Check for actual merge conflict markers (exactly 7 characters followed by space or newline)
392	        conflicts=$(find . -type f ! -path "./.git/*" ! -path "./cli/.git/*" ! -name "lefthook.yml" \
393	          ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.svelte-kit/*" ! -path "./cli/internal/server/frontend/build/*" \
394	          ! -path "./website/public/*" ! -path "./cli/build/*" \
395	          -exec grep -l "^<<<<<<<[ \t]\|^=======\$\|^>>>>>>>[ \t]\|^||||||[ \t]" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -1)
396	        if [ -n "$conflicts" ]; then
397	          echo "Merge conflict markers found in: $conflicts"
398	          exit 1
399	        fi
400	
401	    # Full secrets scan
402	    secrets-all:
403	      <<: *security_checks
404	      name: "Full secrets scan"
405	      run: |
406	        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
407	          gitleaks detect --verbose --redact
408	        else
409	          echo "Consider installing gitleaks for comprehensive secret scanning"
410	        fi
411	
412	    # Go full test suite
413	    go-test-all:
414	      <<: *go_checks
415	      name: "Full Go test suite"
416	      root: "cli/"
417	      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'
418	
419	    bead-schema:
420	      <<: *go_checks
421	      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
422	      run: 'make bead-schema'
423	
424	    skill-schema:
425	      <<: *go_checks
426	      name: "Skill Metadata Guard"
427	      run: 'make skill-schema'
428	
429	    # Go formatting check all files
430	    go-fmt-all:
431	      <<: *go_checks
432	      name: "Check all Go formatting"
433	      root: "cli/"
434	      run: |
435	        unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
436	        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
437	          echo "Unformatted files: $unformatted"
438	          exit 1
439	        fi
440	
441	    # Go lint all files
442	    go-lint-all:
443	      <<: *go_checks
444	      name: "Lint all Go files"
445	      root: "cli/"
446	      run: |
447	        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
448	          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
449	          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
450	          exit 1
451	        fi
452	        golangci-lint run --timeout=5m
453	
454	    # Go build all targets
455	    go-build-all:
456	      <<: *go_checks
457	      name: "Build all targets"
458	      root: "cli/"
459	      run: 'go build -v ./...'
460	
461	    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
462	    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
463	    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
464	    evidence-lint-all:
465	      <<: *go_checks
466	      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
467	      root: "cli/"
468	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'
469	
470	    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
471	    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
472	    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
473	    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
474	    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
475	    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
476	    runtime-lint-all:
477	      <<: *go_checks
478	      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
479	      root: "cli/"
480	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'
481	
482	    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
483	    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
484	    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
485	    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
486	    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
487	    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
488	    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
489	    # cli/ Go source.
490	    routing-lint-all:
491	      <<: *go_checks
492	      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
493	      root: "cli/"
494	      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'
495	
496	    docs-routing-lint:
497	      name: "Docs Routing Lint (legacy DDx routing surfaces allowlisted)"
498	      run: 'bash scripts/check-legacy-routing-docs.sh'
499	
500	    # YAML validation
501	    yaml-validate:
502	      name: "Validate YAML files"
503	      glob: "**/*.{yml,yaml}"
504	      run: |
505	        if command -v yq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
506	          for file in {all_files}; do
507	            yq eval . "$file" >/dev/null || exit 1
508	          done
509	        fi
510	
511	# ====================
512	# Pre-push hooks - Extended validation
513	# ====================
514	pre-push:
515	  parallel: false
516	
517	  commands:
518	    git-config-health:
519	      name: "Git config health"
520	      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
521	      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before pushing."
522	      tags: [policy]
523	
524	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
525	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
526	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
527	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
528	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
529	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
530	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
531	    merge-policy:
532	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
533	      run: |
534	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
535	        remote="${1:-origin}"
536	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
537	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
538	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
539	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
540	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
541	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
542	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
543	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
544	          exit 0
545	        fi
546	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
547	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
548	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
549	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
550	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
551	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
552	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
553	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
554	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
555	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
556	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
557	          echo ""
558	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
559	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
560	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
561	          exit 1
562	        fi
563	        exit 0
564	      tags: [policy]
565	
566	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
567	    no-debug:
568	      name: "No debug statements"
569	      run: |
570	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
571	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
572	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
573	          status=$?
574	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
575	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
576	            exit 0
577	          fi
578	          exit 1
579	        fi
580	
581	# Skip configuration
582	# Usage: LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test,secrets git commit
583	# Or: LEFTHOOK=0 git commit (skip all hooks)
584	
585	# Install hooks: lefthook install
586	# Run manually: lefthook run pre-commit
587	# Run CI checks: lefthook run ci
588

data.text: All current dirs have SKILL.md. Now I'll add the `skill-dir-anchor` hook to lefthook.yml.

input.old_string:
no-tmp-in-test-factory:
      name: "Guard NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") in tests (ddx-9063101c)"
      glob: "cli/cmd/*_test.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/cmd/[^/]+_test\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs grep -lF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")' 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$bad" ]; then
          printf '%s\n' "$bad" | xargs grep -nF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")'
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") found in cli/cmd/*_test.go — use t.TempDir() instead"
input.new_string:
no-tmp-in-test-factory:
      name: "Guard NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") in tests (ddx-9063101c)"
      glob: "cli/cmd/*_test.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/cmd/[^/]+_test\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs grep -lF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")' 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$bad" ]; then
          printf '%s\n' "$bad" | xargs grep -nF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")'
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") found in cli/cmd/*_test.go — use t.TempDir() instead"

    skill-dir-anchor:
      name: "Skill dirs must have SKILL.md"
      run: |
        failed=0
        for base in .agents/skills .claude/skills; do
          [ -d "$base" ] || continue
          for d in $(ls -A "$base"); do
            full="$base/$d"
            [ -d "$full" ] || continue
            if [ ! -f "$full/SKILL.md" ]; then
              echo "ERROR: skill-dir $full has no SKILL.md; skill directories must be real skills with a SKILL.md anchor. If this is contamination, remove the directory. If this is a new skill, add a SKILL.md."
              failed=1
            fi
          done
        done
        exit $failed
      fail_text: "Skill directory missing SKILL.md. Either add SKILL.md (new skill) or remove the directory (contamination)."

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(lint): pre-commit guard rejects skill dirs without SKILL.md [ddx-e94e3e08]

Add skill-dir-anchor pre-commit hook to lefthook.yml that iterates all
direct subdirectories of .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/ using ls -A
(capturing hidden dirs like .ddx/, .github/) and fails the commit if any
lack a SKILL.md anchor file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed. All 6 ACs are satisfied:

1. `lefthook.yml` now contains `skill-dir-anchor` asserting every direct subdirectory of `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` has a `SKILL.md`.
2. The hook uses `ls -A` so hidden dirs (`.ddx/`, `.github/`, etc.) are not skipped.
3. The hook fails with a clear error message naming each offending directory.
4. Manual test 1 (visible `.agents/skills/contamination/`): hook failed with the expected error.
5. Manual test 2 (hidden `.agents/skills/.contamination/`): hook also failed with the expected error.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes on the current cleaned repo.
